title: alnus sibirica f. glabra
intro: Alnus sibirica f. glabra, also known as the glabrous Siberian alder, is a variety of the deciduous tree Alnus sibirica, native to Siberia and the Russian Far East. The bark of this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, making it useful for treating various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Alnus sibirica f. glabra include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and support cardiovascular health. Alnus sibirica f. glabra can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions for its medicinal uses, and it is also used in traditional medicine to make ointments and salves for topical applications.
